raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1237, the 'California Public Interest Veterinary Debt Relief Act,' aims to mitigate the shortage of veterinarians in California.",
    "AB 1237 will offer payments of up to $150,000 in educational debt relief to licensed California veterinarians who work in underserved communities for at least five years.",
    "Veterinary school debt in California averages nearly $200,000.",
    "California shelters are struggling to provide or access veterinary care for vulnerable pets.",
    "Veterinarians have the second highest monthly debt-to-income ratio among graduate degree holders.",
    "The average veterinary school debt reported by an American Veterinary Medical Association (AVMA) survey in 2020 was $188,853.",
    "Educational debt for veterinary graduates is increasing by nearly $6,000 each year.",
    "Less than half of California animal shelters can consistently provide treatment for non-routine illnesses or injuries that require a veterinarian's assessment.",
    "40% of California animal shelter respondents are unable to consistently perform legally required spay/neuter surgeries.",
    "60% of open shelter veterinary positions in California remain vacant due to a lack of candidates.",
    "A lack of access to basic care is leading to an increased length of stay for animals in California shelters."
  ]
}
```
